zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe於1266年出生在英國多瑟特內特萊可姆時，她的父親Warin De Raleigh21歲，母親Joanna Boteler22歲。她於1284年與Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX在英國薩默塞特內特萊可姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1318年在英國薩默塞特阿生頓享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe于1266年出生在英国多瑟特內特萊可姆时，她的父亲Warin De Raleigh21岁，母亲Joanna Boteler22岁。她于1284年与Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX在英国薩默塞特內特萊可姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1318年在英国薩默塞特阿生頓享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Raleigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Raleigh (House) in Pilton (Devon), recorded as Radeleia in 1086 and Rawlegh in 1535. The placename probably derives from Old English rǣge ‘roe deer’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The English explorer Sir Walter Raleigh (1554–1618) was born in Hayes Barton, Devon, into a family of Devon gentry. He was related to most of the West Country's important families, including that of Sir Francis Drake. His half-brother was the explorer Sir Humphrey Gilbert. In 1578 Raleigh was granted a patent to explore and colonize “unknown lands” in America. In the 1580s he was granted extensive estates in Munster (Ireland), in reward for his suppression of the Desmond Rebellions.

variants.name: Rawleigh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Orlo.
English: variant of   Raleigh  , reflecting a common pronunciation.

variants.name: Riley
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name chiefly from High Ryley in Accrington (Lancashire) and Riley in Kirkburton, which was in the manor of Wakefield (Yorkshire). In some cases the name may derive from Riley Farm in Eyam (Derbyshire), Royley in Royton (Lancashire), or Rylah in Scarcliffe (Derbyshire). The placenames all come from Old English rȳge ‘rye’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

Irish: from Ó Raghailligh ‘descendant of Raghailleach’, Old Irish Roghallach. This is the name of a chieftain family in Cavan, related to the O'Rourkes. In Anglicized form it has also been confused with Ó Raithile found in Munster, usually Anglicized as O'Rahilly or Rahilly . See O'Reilly .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe was born in 1266, in Nettlecombe, Dorset, England, her father, Warin De Raleigh, was 21 and her mother, Joanna Boteler, was 22. She married Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX in 1284, in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. She died in 1318, in Ashington, Somerset, England, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe est née en 1266, à Nettlecombe, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Warin De Raleigh, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Joanna Boteler, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX en 1284, à Nettlecombe, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1318, à Ashington, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe 1266 in Nettlecombe, Dorset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Warin De Raleigh 21 und ihre Mutter Joanna Boteler 22. Sie hat 1284 in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1318 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Ashington, Somerset, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe è nata il nel 1266 nel Nettlecombe, Dorset, England, sua padre, Warin De Raleigh, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Joanna Boteler, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX nel 1284, nel Nettlecombe, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1318, nel Ashington, Somerset, England, all'età di 52 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe(이)가 1266년 영국 도르세트 네틀렉브에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Warin De Raleigh(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joanna Boteler(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1284년 영국 서머세트 네틀렉브에서 Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1318년 영국 서머세트 애신그턴에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe nasceu em 1266, em Nettlecombe, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Warin De Raleigh, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Joanna Boteler, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX em 1284, em Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1318, em Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe родилась в 1266 г. в местности неттлекомб, дорсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Warin De Raleigh было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Joanna Boteler, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX в 1284 г. в местности неттлекомб, сомирсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1318 г. в местности ашингтон, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe nació en 1266, en Nettlecombe, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, Warin De Raleigh, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Joanna Boteler, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX en 1284, en Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió en 1318, en Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 52 años.
